Mirrors  >  Home  >  MPE Home  >  Th. List  >  weq Structured version   Visualization version   GIF version
Theorem weq 1982
Description: Extend wff definition to include atomic formulas using the equality predicate.

(Instead of introducing weq 1982 as an axiomatic statement, as was done in an older version of this database, we introduce it by "proving" a special case of set theory's more general wceq 1560. This lets us avoid overloading the = connective, thus preventing ambiguity that would complicate certain Metamath parsers. However, logically weq 1982 is considered to be a primitive syntax, even though here it is artificially "derived" from wceq 1560. Note: To see the proof steps of this syntax proof, type "MM> SHOW PROOF weq / ALL" in the Metamath program.) (Contributed by NM, 24-Jan-2006.)

Assertion
Ref Expression
weq wff 𝑥 = 𝑦
Proof of Theorem weq
StepHypRef Expression
1 wceq 1560 1 wff 𝑥 = 𝑦
Colors of variables: wff setvar class
Syntax hints:   = wceq 1560
